definitions: An intergovernmental organization established in the framework of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) in 1974 after the 1973 oil crisis. It acts as a policy adviser for its member states, but also works with non-member countries, especially China, India, and Russia.
The Institute of Economic Affairs, a free-market think tank based in London, United Kingdom.
examples: The IEA released its monthly oil market report.
Membership in the IEA has grown over the years.
